<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check state graph for all states and their properties
print(f"Current state: {state_graph.current}")
print(f"Frontier: {state_graph.frontier()}")

# Check all states in the graph
all_states = set()
for entry in history:
    sid = entry.frame.segmentation  # This won't work
    pass

# Let me check what states we've visited
print(f"\nHistory length: {len(history)}")
for i, entry in enumerate(history):
    seg = entry.frame.segmentation
    green = len([n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'])
    print(f"  Step {i}: {entry.action}, green={green}")

# Check what actions are tried from current state
print(f"\nTried actions from current state: {state_graph.tried_actions(state_graph.current)}")
print(f"Neighbors of current state: {state_graph.neighbors(state_graph.current)}")

# Check if there are any unvisited states that might be the goal
# Look at the state graph structure
for sid in range(20):  # Check first 20 states
    try:
        neighbors = state_graph.neighbors(sid)
        if neighbors:
            print(f"State {sid}: neighbors={neighbors}")
    except:
        pass
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I'm in state 11. Let me check what valid actions are available and try some
# First, let me see what MOUSE positions are in the grid area
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"Current state: {state_graph.current}")

# The grid area seems to be rows 10-50, cols 9-50 or so
# Let me try clicking on some cells in the grid to see if any lead to new states
# Focus on cells that might be targets or interactive

# Try clicking on white cells in the grid
white_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w' and min(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) >= 12]
print(f"White nodes in grid: {len(white_nodes)}")

# Try a few white cells to see if any lead to new states
for n in white_nodes[:3]:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': min_r, 'col': min_c}])
    print(f"Clicked ({min_r},{min_c}): state={last_action_result.get('state_node_id')}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
    if last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed'):
        print(f"  -> New state reached!")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

    sid = entry.frame.segmentation  # This won't work
    pass

# Let me check what states we've visited
print(f"\nHistory length: {len(history)}")
for i, entry in enumerate(history):
    seg = entry.frame.segmentation
    green = len([n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'])
    print(f"  Step {i}: {entry.action}, green={green}")

# Check what actions are tried from current state
print(f"\nTried actions from current state: {state_graph.tried_actions(state_graph.current)}")
print(f"Neighbors of current state: {state_graph.neighbors(state_graph.current)}")

# Check if there are any unvisited states that might be the goal
# Look at the state graph structure
for sid in range(20):  # Check first 20 states
    try:
        neighbors = state_graph.neighbors(sid)
        if neighbors:
            print(f"State {sid}: neighbors={neighbors}")
    except:
        pass
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I'm in state 11. Let me check what valid actions are available and try some
# First, let me see what MOUSE positions are in the grid area
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"Current state: {state_graph.current}")

# The grid area seems to be rows 10-50, cols 9-50 or so
# Let me try clicking on some cells in the grid to see if any lead to new states
# Focus on cells that might be targets or interactive

# Try clicking on white cells in the grid
white_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w' and min(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) >= 12]
print(f"White nodes in grid: {len(white_nodes)}")

# Try a few white cells to see if any lead to new states
for n in white_nodes[:3]:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': min_r, 'col': min_c}])
    print(f"Clicked ({min_r},{min_c}): state={last_action_result.get('state_node_id')}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
    if last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed'):
        print(f"  -> New state reached!")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
